text:
There was a girl named Linda.

One summer Linda decided she wanted something unusual. She embarked on collecting feathers.

With determination, she wandered forests, parks, and fields. Linda found beautiful feathers everywhere: owls' eyes, peacock tails, night sparrows'. Some were impossible colors.

Once her collection grew impressive enough to put in a photo album—an owl's eye!

She hung it on her bedroom wall. Every morning she would peek.

Her grandmother noticed. "That feather owl?" Grandma teased.

"It seems wise," Linda said.

"Just lazy." Grandma shook her head.

One day Linda saw something extraordinary. A big hawk swooped with a red streak. It stole two feathers!

She chased it. What should happen next?

That's a story for another time...<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
